span.p:
Fire at every state transition
+ scheduled reporter runs

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Per-record signals fire at record closed

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Sample-level signals fire at AUDIT CADENCE
(15% rolling / first-100 / 30% embedded-summary override):
no_silent_mode_drift_in_sample,
no_passive_promotion_to_affirmed_in_sample,
redaction-attestation, altitude-consent

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Fire at Charter fields-completed
+ mode-migration events

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Truth value cannot change
between Charter-state events

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Emission frequency is matched to the rate
at which signal truth can actually change.

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
COUNT FLAG (cross-stream reconciliation): v5.1.0 substrate count = 23 signals
(6 L1 + 13 L2 + 4 L3), newer than the manifest's 21-token snapshot.
Displayed counts are flagged for reconciliation, not asserted as final.
